title: Maple pecan tart
subtitle: A shortcrust tart filled with a set maple and pecan custard
excerpt: A maple pecan tart has a short pastry case filled with a custard of maple syrup, eggs, and butter, with pecans arranged in the top layer. When baked, the custard sets firm enough to slice but remains slightly soft and sticky in the centre. The flavour is deeply sweet with a caramel note from the maple.
sourceNotes: Pecan tart or pecan pie is an American baking tradition documented from the nineteenth century, particularly in the American South, where pecans are a native crop. The maple syrup version substitutes maple for corn syrup and is well established in North American baking. The form here follows standard public-domain recipes for baked nut tarts.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake and leave to cool completely at room temperature before slicing. Keeps 2 days at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the custard filling needs a low temperature to set without bubbling

content.text: A maple pecan tart is a blind-baked shortcrust case filled with a sweet, set custard of maple syrup, eggs, and butter, with pecan halves pressed into the top. The custard bakes slowly at 170°C, setting from the outside in; the centre should still wobble slightly when the tart comes out of the oven and firms as it cools. It must cool completely before slicing.

Rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolk and cold water and bring together into a firm dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll to 3 mm thick and line a 23 cm tart tin with a removable base. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Heat the oven to 190°C fan. Line the pastry with baking paper, fill with baking beans, and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is pale gold and matte. Reduce the oven to 170°C.

Whisk the maple syrup, eggs, melted butter, brown sugar, and vanilla together until combined. Arrange the pecan halves in the blind-baked case in an even layer. Pour the custard carefully over them; the pecans will float and arrange themselves. Bake at 170°C for 28 to 32 minutes. The tart is done when the edges are set and the centre still has a slight wobble when the tray is shaken. Cool completely at room temperature before slicing; the filling firms to a sliceable set only when cold.
